[View commit history for this file.](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/commits/main/) 2,028 Commits | | [.github](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/tree/main/.github ".github") | [.github](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/tree/main/.github ".github") | [ci: reorganize CI into a single workflow](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/commit/0697349126f955abf65ae9e4f5fc7daf75af2115 "ci: reorganize CI into a single workflow Details: - This centralizes all quality control checks in place. - This allows creating a single \"workflow status\" job. It is useful as an indicator in various scenarios: - Required check in branch protection. Instead of listing and maintaining all jobs in branch protection ruleset, list them in workflow. - Act as a trigger for potential future synchronization jobs: for dual distribution and for site.") | 3 months agoDec 20, 2025 | | [benchmarks/starter](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/tree/main/benchmarks/starter "This path skips through empty directories") | [benchmarks/starter](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/tree/main/benchmarks/starter "This path skips through empty directories") | [docs!: transfer to GitHub organization](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/commit/07ab5a4dcd6a82ec31426a40f8f7e1fdce442934 "docs!: transfer to GitHub organization Details: - This should not really be a breaking change (as GitHub should redirect from personal account to the new org), but it requires extra attention for users to adjust their config.") | 7 months agoAug 28, 2025 | | [colors](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/tree/main/colors "colors") | [colors](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/tree/main/colors "colors") | [docs(ALL): adjust lower/upper letters in "Mini\*" words](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/commit/f82197dafd89bbb65d758f35caa354c12881b50f "docs(ALL): adjust lower/upper letters in \"Mini*\" words") | 4 months agoNov 17, 2025 | | [doc](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/tree/main/doc "doc") | [doc](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/tree/main/doc "doc") | [fix(pick)!: don't force redraw every `config.delay.async` milliseconds](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/commit/7ff1d3073fde4b98f41bbfc383d5f06932f8d6fa "fix(pick)!: don't force redraw every `config.delay.async` milliseconds Details: - Forcing redraw every fixed amount of milliseconds was primarily added to avoid possible confusion from users when something is not shown \"in the background\", since `getcharstr()` blocks any redraw. The 10ms results in essentially 100 FPS responsiveness, which is smooth. However, this is a bit too much as it can result into constant 1-2% CPU load just when picker does nothing. This is not the biggest load, but it adds up and can have visible effect if the picker is left open in the background after switching to a different process. - A positive side effect of this constant redraw is that preview with async syntax highlighting (like after `vim.treesitter.start()`) is perceived as shown \"immediately\" (seen only in `builtin.help` picker, though). While not forcing redraw might show initial preview without highlighting until some key is pressed. Document that it needs to be done explicitly, like after small delay. - Increasing default `delay.async` value to something like 100ms is possible, but this will make it \"less smooth\" of a feeling to type when the picker is busy *and* will probably flicker with async previewing. The best solution is to make upstream Neovim PR to allow `getchar()` / `getcharstr()` to not block redraw (at least behind the new option). Related to #2306") | yesterdayMar 17, 2026 | | [lua/mini](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/tree/main/lua/mini "This path skips through empty directories") | [lua/mini](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/tree/main/lua/mini "This path skips through empty directories") | [refactor(jump): simplify the main `jump` function](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/commit/28d993854bdcde7d06f0990cfe6a2c6577f3f8ab "refactor(jump): simplify the main `jump` function Details: - Instead of always checking if target exists in text, only do so if there was no jump. Additional benefit: the code using the cursor to check if a jump occurred can be replaced by a more direct check. - Rearrange code for a lower LOC and more clarity. Co-authored-by: Evgeni Chasnovski ") | yesterdayMar 17, 2026 | | [readmes](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/tree/main/readmes "readmes") | [readmes](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/tree/main/readmes "readmes") | [feat(jump)!: make dot-repeat jumping less intefere with regular jumping](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/commit/0098de999048af0539183d625c52d733318a441b "feat(jump)!: make dot-repeat jumping less intefere with regular jumping Resolve #2054 Co-authored-by: Evgeni Chasnovski ") | 3 weeks agoFeb 28, 2026 | | [scripts](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/tree/main/scripts "scripts") | [scripts](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/tree/main/scripts "scripts") | [ci: update 'lintcommit' to ignore length of "Co-authored-by:" lines](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/commit/b2d66f958aeefd3b7061d735c6a6ead014f76fec "ci: update 'lintcommit' to ignore length of \"Co-authored-by:\" lines") | 2 months agoJan 9, 2026 | | [tests](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/tree/main/tests "tests") | [tests](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/tree/main/tests "tests") | [fix(pick)!: don't force redraw every `config.delay.async` milliseconds](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/commit/7ff1d3073fde4b98f41bbfc383d5f06932f8d6fa "fix(pick)!: don't force redraw every `config.delay.async` milliseconds Details: - Forcing redraw every fixed amount of milliseconds was primarily added to avoid possible confusion from users when something is not shown \"in the background\", since `getcharstr()` blocks any redraw. The 10ms results in essentially 100 FPS responsiveness, which is smooth. However, this is a bit too much as it can result into constant 1-2% CPU load just when picker does nothing. This is not the biggest load, but it adds up and can have visible effect if the picker is left open in the background after switching to a different process. - A positive side effect of this constant redraw is that preview with async syntax highlighting (like after `vim.treesitter.start()`) is perceived as shown \"immediately\" (seen only in `builtin.help` picker, though). While not forcing redraw might show initial preview without highlighting until some key is pressed. Document that it needs to be done explicitly, like after small delay. - Increasing default `delay.async` value to something like 100ms is possible, but this will make it \"less smooth\" of a feeling to type when the picker is busy *and* will probably flicker with async previewing. The best solution is to make upstream Neovim PR to allow `getchar()` / `getcharstr()` to not block redraw (at least behind the new option). [README.md](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/blob/main/README.md "README.md") | [README.md](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/blob/main/README.md "README.md") | [fix(ALL): clarify that a Clarify further that this is just a reminder in the message itself and in the README.") | last monthFeb 17, 2026 | | [TESTING.md](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/blob/main/TESTING.md "TESTING.md") | [TESTING.md](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/blob/main/TESTING.md "TESTING.md") | [docs(ALL): tweak markdown files for better portability](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/commit/0d7629a40e7f0e16d0853f7e164c549893af9405 "docs(ALL): tweak markdown files for better portability Details: - Use

instead of

to center align logo. - Use explicit `style=\"max-width:100%;border:solid 2px\"` in logos to limit width and add border. Border doesn't have effect on GitHub, but it makes logos stand out more on the future site. This implies that a subtle difference in background color is not a mistake. - Reorganize installation instructions to be regular Markdown tables instead of a complicated HTML with merged cells. - Use \"Documentation\" to link to help files (instead of \"help file\"). - Use direct links to 'nvim-mini/assets' as much as possible: - For images it works as is. - For videos, unfortunately, it is not possible (hopefully yet) to embed by using something like https://github.com/nvim-mini/assets/blob/main/demo/demo-ai.mp4?raw=true directly (with or without `raw=true`). The only way is still to drag and drop a video in site's Markdown editor, get a link, and use it directly. But for reference add a comment with link to source. - Add blank line before list for more robust list detection (not only on GitHub). - Use raw HTML tags as little as possible. Like prefer native Markdown list item continuation instead of
. - Prefer \"`:h MiniXxx`\" to link to help tag instead of saying \"`*MiniXxx*` help tag\".") | 6 months agoSep 26, 2025 | | [logo.png](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/blob/main/logo.png "logo.png") | [logo.png](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/blob/main/logo.png "logo.png") | [docs: rebrand logos](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/commit/fc7eec8f2cf349a3f5449ad2d8a482d9043b7a1e "docs: rebrand logos") | 8 months agoJul 22, 2025 | | View all files | ## Repository files navigation - [README](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#) - [Code of conduct](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#) - [Contributing](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#) - [MIT license](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#) [![mini.nvim](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/raw/main/logo.png)](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/blob/main/logo.png) ### All-in-one plugin [Permalink: All-in-one plugin](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#all-in-one-plugin) Library of 40+ independent Lua modules improving overall [Neovim](https://github.com/neovim/neovim) (version 0.9 and higher) experience with minimal effort. They all share same configuration approaches and general design principles. Think about this project as "Swiss Army knife" among Neovim plugins: it has many different independent tools (modules) suitable for most common tasks. Each module can be used separately without any startup and usage overhead. If you want to help this project grow but don't know where to start: - Leave a Github star for 'mini.nvim' and/or any other [MINI projects](https://nvim-mini.org/). - Check out [contributing guides](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md). See [change log](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md) for a history of changes, including current development version. See [MiniMax](https://nvim-mini.org/MiniMax) for a full config example based on 'mini.nvim'. Note This was previously hosted at `echasnovski/mini.nvim`. It was transferred to a dedicated organization to improve long term project stability. See more details [here](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/discussions/1970). ## Table of contents [Permalink: Table of contents](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#table-of-contents) - [Installation](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#installation) - [Modules](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#modules) - [General principles](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#general-principles) - [Plugin color schemes](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#plugin-color-schemes) - [Planned modules](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#planned-modules) ## Installation [Permalink: Installation](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#installation) There are two branches to install from: - `main` (default, **recommended**) will have latest development version of plugin. All changes since last stable release should be perceived as being in beta testing phase (meaning they already passed alpha-testing and are moderately settled). - `stable` will be updated only upon releases with code tested during public beta-testing phase in `main` branch. Here are code snippets for some common installation methods: - Manually with `git clone` (compatible with [mini.deps](https://nvim-mini.org/mini.nvim/readmes/mini-deps)): ``` -- Put this at the top of 'init.lua' local path_package = vim.fn.stdpath('data') .. '/site' local mini_path = path_package .. '/pack/deps/start/mini.nvim' if not vim.loop.fs_stat(mini_path) then vim.cmd('echo "Installing `mini.nvim`" | redraw') local clone_cmd = { 'git', 'clone', '--filter=blob:none', -- Uncomment next line to use 'stable' branch -- '--branch', 'stable', 'https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim', mini_path } vim.fn.system(clone_cmd) vim.cmd('packadd mini.nvim | helptags ALL') vim.cmd('echo "Installed `mini.nvim`" | redraw') end ``` - With [folke/lazy.nvim](https://github.com/folke/lazy.nvim): | Branch | Code snippet | | --- | --- | | Main | `{ 'nvim-mini/mini.nvim', version = false },` | | Stable | `{ 'nvim-mini/mini.nvim', version = '*' },` | - With [junegunn/vim-plug](https://github.com/junegunn/vim-plug): | Branch | Code snippet | | --- | --- | | Main | `Plug 'nvim-mini/mini.nvim'` | | Stable | `Plug 'nvim-mini/mini.nvim', { 'branch': 'stable' }` | - Every module is also distributed as a standalone Git repository. Check out module's information for more details. **Important**: don't forget to call module's `setup()` (if required) to enable its functionality. **Note**: if you are on Windows, there might be problems with too long file paths (like `error: unable to create file : Filename too long`). Try doing one of the following: - Enable corresponding git global config value: `git config --system core.longpaths true`. Then try to reinstall. - Install plugin in other place with shorter path. ## Modules [Permalink: Modules](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#modules) 'mini.nvim' contains many modules which is slightly daunting at first. All of them can be used independently, one at a time. For easier exploration, here they are presented in groups based on module's primary functionality (although some modules can fit in several groups). See more detailed listing [here](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/blob/main/doc/mini-nvim.txt). ### Text editing [Permalink: Text editing](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#text-editing) These modules improve your text editing experience. [Permalink: General principles](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#general-principles) ### Design [Permalink: Design](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#design) Each module is designed to solve a particular problem targeting balance between feature-richness (handling as many edge-cases as possible) and simplicity of implementation/support. Granted, not all of them ended up with the same balance, but it is the goal nevertheless. ### Independence [Permalink: Independence](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#independence) Modules are independent of each other and can be run without external dependencies. Although some of them may need dependencies for full experience. ### Structure [Permalink: Structure](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#structure) Each module is a submodule for a placeholder "mini" module. So, for example, "surround" module should be referred to as "mini.surround". As later will be explained, this plugin can also be referred to as "MiniSurround". ### Setup [Permalink: Setup](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#setup) - Each module you want to use should be enabled separately with `require().setup({})`. Possibly replace `{}` with your config table or omit altogether to use defaults. You can supply only parts of config, the rest will be inferred from defaults. - Call to module's `setup()` always creates a global Lua object with coherent camel-case name: `require('mini.surround').setup()` creates `_G.MiniSurround`. This allows for a simpler usage of plugin functionality: instead of `require('mini.surround')` use `MiniSurround` (or manually `:lua MiniSurround.*` in command line); available from `v:lua` like `v:lua.MiniSurround`. Considering this, "module" and "Lua object" names can be used interchangeably: 'mini.surround' and 'MiniSurround' will mean the same thing. - Each supplied `config` table is stored in `config` field of global object. Like `MiniSurround.config`. - Values of `config` which affect runtime activity can be changed on the fly to have effect. For example, `MiniSurround.config.n_lines` can be changed during runtime; but changing `MiniSurround.config.mappings` won't have any effect (as mappings are created once during `setup()`). - If module works best with some specific non-default option value, it is set during `setup()`. If the value is not essential to module's functionality, it is done only if user or another plugin hasn't set it beforehand (no matter the value). ### Buffer local configuration [Permalink: Buffer local configuration](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#buffer-local-configuration) Each module can be additionally configured to use certain runtime config settings locally to buffer. See `mini.nvim-buffer-local-config` section in help file for more information. ### Buffer names [Permalink: Buffer names](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#buffer-names) All module-related buffers are named according to the following format: `mini:///` (forward slashes are used on any platform; `` may be empty). This structure allows creating identifiable, reasonably unique, and useful buffer names. For example, 'mini.files' buffers are created per displayed directory/file with names like `minifiles://10/path/to/displayed/directory`. ### Disabling [Permalink: Disabling](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#disabling) Each module's core functionality can be disabled globally or locally to buffer. See "Disabling" section in module's help page for more details. See `mini.nvim-disabling-recipes` section in main help file for common recipes. ### Silencing [Permalink: Silencing](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#silencing) Each module providing non-error feedback (like a reminder to press a key after some idle time in 'mini.ai', 'mini.jump2d', 'mini.surround') can be configured to not do that by setting `config.silent = true` (either inside `setup()` call or on the fly). ### Highlighting [Permalink: Highlighting](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#highlighting) Appearance of module's output is controlled by certain set of highlight groups (see `:h highlight-groups`). By default they usually link to some semantically close built-in highlight group and are ensured to be defined after any color scheme takes effect. Use `:highlight` command or `vim.api.nvim_set_hl()` Lua function to customize highlighting. To see a more calibrated look, use 'mini.hues', 'mini.base16', or plugin's color scheme. ### Stability [Permalink: Stability](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#stability) Each module upon release is considered to be relatively stable: both in terms of setup and functionality. Any non-bugfix backward-incompatible change will be released gradually as much as possible. ### Not filetype and language specific [Permalink: Not filetype and language specific](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#not-filetype-and-language-specific) Including functionality which needs several filetype/language specific implementations is an explicit no-goal of this project. This is mostly due to the potential increase in maintenance to keep implementation up to date. However, any part which might need filetype/language specific tuning should be designed to allow it by letting user set proper buffer options and/or local configuration. ## Plugin color schemes [Permalink: Plugin color schemes](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#plugin-color-schemes) This plugin comes with several color schemes (all have both dark and light variants). Activate any of them as a regular `colorscheme` (like `:colorscheme miniwinter` or `vim.cmd.colorscheme('miniwinter')`). - Based on ['mini.hues'](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/blob/main/readmes/mini-hues.md) (recommended): - `miniwinter`: "icy winter" palette with azure background. - `minispring`: "blooming spring" palette with green background. - `minisummer`: "hot summer" palette with brown/yellow background. - `miniautumn`: "cooling autumn" palette with purple background. - `randomhue`: random background and foreground of the same hue with medium saturation. You can see how they look in ['mini.hues' README](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/blob/main/readmes/mini-hues.md#bundled-color-schemes). - Based on ['mini.base16'](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/blob/main/readmes/mini-base16.md): - `minicyan`: cyan and grey main colors with medium contrast and saturation palette. - `minischeme`: blue and yellow main colors with high contrast and saturation palette. You can see how they look in ['mini.base16' README](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/blob/main/readmes/mini-base16.md#demo). ## Planned modules [Permalink: Planned modules](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#planned-modules) This is the list of modules I currently intend to implement eventually (as my free time and dedication will allow), in alphabetical order: - 'mini.abbrev' - helper to manage/setup Insert mode abbreviations. - 'mini.cycle' - cycle through alternatives with pre-defined rules. Something like [monaqa/dial.nvim](https://github.com/monaqa/dial.nvim) and [AndrewRadev/switch.vim](https://github.com/AndrewRadev/switch.vim) - 'mini.folds' - more capable and user-friendly folds. - 'mini.repl' - extendable wrapper for REPLs with built-in support for R, Python, Julia, and maybe (just maybe) some AI tools. - 'mini.sendtext' - send text between buffers. In particular between regular and built-in terminal buffers. - 'mini.statuscolumn' - customizable 'statuscolumn'. - 'mini.terminals' - coherently manage interactive terminal buffers. Something like [kassio/neoterm](https://github.com/kassio/neoterm). Might also incorporate functionality to asynchronously run code in shell with post-processed results. - 'mini.quickfix' - more capable and user-friendly quickfix list. Possibly with preview and inline editing for search-and-replace workflow. - 'mini.windows' - window manager. Interactive picker, layout organizer, and maybe more. ## About Library of 40+ independent Lua modules improving Neovim experience with minimal effort [nvim-mini.org/mini.nvim/](https://nvim-mini.org/mini.nvim/ "https://nvim-mini.org/mini.nvim/") ### Topics [lua](https://github.com/topics/lua "Topic: lua") [neovim](https://github.com/topics/neovim "Topic: neovim") [neovim-plugin](https://github.com/topics/neovim-plugin "Topic: neovim-plugin") [mini-nvim](https://github.com/topics/mini-nvim "Topic: mini-nvim") ### Resources [Readme](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#readme-ov-file) ### License [MIT license](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#MIT-1-ov-file) ### Code of conduct [Code of conduct](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#coc-ov-file) ### Contributing [Contributing](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim#contributing-ov-file) ### Uh oh!
